introduction: As Lee's Market expands its footprint across US and Canada, protecting our digital infrastructure from security threats becomes paramount. This presentation outlines our strategy for implementing a robust cybersecurity awareness and training program by 2025 to mitigate risks associated with high employee turnover and seasonal hiring.

subsections.heading: Current State of Cybersecurity at Lee's Market
subsections.content:
• During the peak holiday season in December 2024, a security training gap was observed as only slightly above half of the new hires were onboarded with cybersecurity training before they received system access.
• This lack of comprehensive training for 47% of new hires during the busiest retail period potentially exposes Lee's Market to increased cybersecurity risks.
• The current training process is inconsistent with industry trends, where 78% of temporary retail employees do not receive social engineering training.
• The gaps in safe internet, social media usage guidance, and mock email phishing training are of particular concern, given VikingCloud's report indicating an industry-wide lack of these critical training components.
• This situation is exacerbated by the fact that 52% of retailers report inadequate internal IT resources to keep up with the evolving cyberattack methods.
• The current state of cybersecurity training not only poses a threat to the operational security but also hinders our growth in the U.S. and Canadian markets.
• A strategic shift in our approach to cybersecurity training is required to ensure the security of our digital and operational infrastructure.
subsections.justification: This content highlights the existing gaps in cybersecurity training at Lee's Market, emphasizing the need for a new cybersecurity awareness and training program to mitigate security risks.

subsections.heading: Challenges in Maintaining Cybersecurity Amidst High Employee Turnover
subsections.content:
• Temporary employees constitute 35% of our workforce annually
• Training temporary employees on cybersecurity best practices is a time-consuming process
• 30% of reported incidents involved temporary employees in 2024
• The high turnover rate affects the consistency of our security measures
• We've seen a rise in phishing attacks targeting temporary employees
• Employee churn increases the risk of internal threats and data leaks
subsections.justification: This content discusses the issues with high employee turnover but doesn't detail how to design and implement a training program, as asked in the DR Question.

subsections.heading: Designing a Cybersecurity Training Program for Sustainable Growth
subsections.content:
• The training program will be developed in line with industry best practices
• Partnerships with external cybersecurity training providers are being considered
• Aiming to implement regular training sessions for all new hires
• Considering an annual refresher course for all employees
• Plans to develop micro-learning modules for easy access and learning
• The cost of developing the training program is estimated to be around $1.5M
subsections.justification: This slide talks about general steps to design a training program, but doesn't provide specific details on mitigating risks or minimizing incidents, which is the focus of the DR Question.

conclusion: Implementing a robust cybersecurity training program is key to safeguarding our digital infrastructure as we grow. Next steps include detailed planning, securing budget approvals, and initiating pilot training sessions in select stores.
